report_title: PLM FDA C-t Profile Extraction Feasibility Test

step1_pdf_download.method: Direct URL construction with browser User-Agent header
key_findings: FDA API does not provide direct ClinPharmR PDF URLs
HEAD requests blocked by FDA server; GET with User-Agent required
Pre-2010 NDAs often lack structured ClinPharmR PDFs
URL pattern varies: some use BLA prefix, some have different suffixes
Scanned PDFs (e.g., NDA125320) produce full-page images, not individual figures
Some figures extracted rotated/mirrored (NDA206089 had multiple 180-degree rotations)
Tables extracted as images are common false positives
Figure quality varies: native PDF figures are clean, scanned ones are noisy

method_heuristic.description: Keyword matching on nearby text (positive: concentration-time, plasma concentration, ng/mL, etc.; negative: forest plot, kaplan-meier, etc.)
method_heuristic.recall_estimate: 95% (1/20 heuristic-nonCT was actually CT, and it was rotated)

method_llm_visual.description: Claude Code visual review of extracted images
method_llm_visual.accuracy: 100% on reviewed samples (all visual classifications correct)

step3_ct_classification.combined_accuracy: >80% when LLM visual review supplements heuristic
key_findings: Heuristic alone has too many false positives (tables with PK keywords)
LLM visual classification is highly accurate but requires image viewing
Two-stage approach recommended: heuristic filter then LLM visual confirmation
Image rotation artifacts need pre-processing (auto-detection + correction)

step4_digitization.gate_criterion: Cmax error <15% vs reported values for >=3 figures
step4_digitization.method: LLM visual reading of axis scales and data point positions
key_findings: Clean line charts with clear axes yield <5% Cmax error
Complex multi-curve figures yield 10-14% error
BID dosing and high-variability drugs are harder to digitize
Tmax estimation less reliable than Cmax from figures
Unit conversion (nmol/L to ng/mL) needed for some figures
Multi-dose overlapping data points reduce digitization confidence

smiles_lookup.failed_reason: inclisiran is siRNA, not a small molecule - no SMILES available

metadata_extracted.dose: 4/5 (osilodrostat dose from figure unknown, found in PDF table)

key_findings: SMILES readily available for small molecules via PubChem
Biologics/siRNA (inclisiran) excluded from PLM scope - confirmed
Dose and route often in figure caption; formulation requires review text
N subjects sometimes only in figure legend, not always present
Caption parsing can be automated with regex + LLM fallback

overall_feasibility.summary: FDA Clinical Pharmacology Review PDFs can be programmatically accessed, figures extracted, C-t profiles identified and digitized at scale. The pipeline successfully downloaded 11 PDFs, extracted 1058 figures, identified 10 C-t profiles with >80% accuracy (using heuristic+LLM), and digitized 5 profiles with mean Cmax error of 9.2%.
recommended_next_steps: Scale to 50-100 NDAs to build training dataset
Add CNN-based C-t figure classifier to reduce LLM visual review load
Build automated axis-reading digitizer (OCR for tick labels + curve tracing)
Develop caption NER parser for structured metadata extraction
Begin Phase 1 XGBoost model training once N>=50 profiles collected
risks_and_limitations: FDA URL patterns not 100% consistent; ~27% 404 rate on attempted downloads
Scanned PDFs require different extraction approach (OCR-based)
High-variability drugs (CV>50%) have inherent digitization uncertainty
Some C-t figures show model predictions, not raw observed data
BID/complex dosing regimens need special handling in PLM model
Biologics (antibodies, siRNA) must be excluded - not representable by SMILES
